RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 306-A, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Bob Titus, a Republican, represents western Christian County (District 139) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2022.
Prior to his legislative duties, Titus was a business owner and agent representing a major multiline insurance company for 30 years. Serving his insurance clients well was a significant source of pride and satisfaction. Bob retired his business upon successfully gaining an opportunity to serve the residents of Missouri House District 139.
Bob and Tammy Titus first moved to Christian County in 1984. They have lived in their current home near Billings since 1994 and there raised their 10 children. God has continued to bless them with the rich addition of six children-in-law and 17 grandchildren. No family gathering is short of a happy crowd.
Bob’s priorities of faith and family are not only a catalyst for government service, they are the qualities that best represent the values of Christian County. Bob uses his 30 years of business and financial service to ensure the best interests of district, county, and state, are well represented in Jefferson City.
Bob has an Accounting degree from Missouri State University. Family occupations and expertise include the fields of business, health, science, first-responders, aviation, and disabilities. The favorite family activity is outdoor recreation.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Urges the U.S. government to condemn human rights violations in North Korea

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Missouri Religious Freedom Protection Act"
Modifies provisions relating to income tax
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Modifies standards for storage and use of gold and silver
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Modifies provisions relating to settlement demands
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Establishes written parental consent requirements for individualized education programs (IEPs)
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Modifies provisions relating to firearms
Prohibits state departments from spending money on diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives
Clarifies the short-term authority of local taxing entities relating to certain property tax rate and property tax rate ceiling adjustments in a year following a voluntary tax rate reduction
Designates the "Lloyd Memorial Highway" in Newton County
Establishes the "Missouri Prenatal Equal Protection Act"
Reestablishes the "Second Amendment Preservation Act"
Prohibits the use of SNAP benefits to purchase certain foods

Proposes a constitutional amendment to impose an appropriation spending limitation and to establish the "Tax Reform Fund" to be used to fund budgetary shortfalls, subject to an appropriation limitation, implement sales tax changes, and allow for certain taxation changes based on revenue triggers, by general law
Proposes a constitutional amendment to prohibit any new tax or increase in tax from going into effect unless approved by the voters in a general election
Proposes a constitutional amendment regarding the right to life
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies provisions relating to constitutional amendments
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to modification of the Constitution
